course_description:
Introduction to marketing management; consumer behaviour; marketing research and segmentation; product life cycle theory; product and pricing strategies; distribution and logistics; promotional strategy including advertising and personal selling; marketing organisation, planning and control; international marketing, services marketing and marketing for not-for-profit organisations.

Fundamentals of Marketingﾠis designed to provide an introduction to central marketing concepts and the current marketing environment. Learners will explore key marketing principles and begin to understand the science ofﾠdecision making involved in marketing practice. The course is designed to provide foundations of marketing theory for extended development and will involve both theoretical development and practical applications. To pass the course, students will need to demonstrate acquisition of specific marketing concepts which will be reviewed each week in the course.

course_aims: In this course students are exposed to the principles of marketing, the marketing planning process and the central areas of marketing. As such, the course provides an introduction to marketing management, consumer behaviour, marketing research, marketing segmentation,ﾠproduct strategies, pricing strategy, distribution strategy andﾠpromotional strategyﾠas related to the marketing mix within an organisation. The course aim to develop awareness of the important concepts ofﾠethics and contemporary themes of marketing relevant in a range of business contexts.

learning_outcomes.description: Communicate an appreciation for the importance of marketing to business success in a range of contexts.
Explain the central role of the customer, whether individual or organisational, in the marketing process.
Evaluate situational influences on marketing across a range of industries and companies.
Collect and analyse evidence to determine competitive advantage, target market and position.
Analyse marketing evidence to evaluate and determine the effectiveness of marketing mix strategies.

assessment_details.title: Marketing quiz
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: An online quiz will be run in Week 5 during the scheduled time, via the course blackboard site, in Assessments>Assessment 1 online quiz. The quiz is designed to engage students in early exploration of marketing theory. The quiz will cover all content from Week 1, 2, and 3 lectures, as well as Week 2, 3 and 4 tutorials and all readings from these weeks (Chapters 1, 2, 3,and 5). Questions and answer orders will be randomly generated. The quiz will be available to complete during the scheduled time only, which is a 24 hour window, where students are to complete the quiz individually and will have 30 minutes to complete the quiz once it is commenced. Further instructions and details will be available in the Assessment folder. All students MUST follow the specific guidelines provided. Students MUST complete the quiz during the scheduled time. Students who fail to follow the guidelines and who have technical issues will be deemed responsible and will incur the outcome. Formal applications are essential for anyone not able to sit - no extension will be provided by course staff - this must be formally managed by students. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: STP Analysis
assessment_details.task_description: The aim of this assessment task is to increase knowledge of the segmenting, targeting and positioning. Learners will perform a review of a designated product brand (an actual real life market offering), examining the marketing theory and the market evidence to determine the current STP. In doing so, learners will need to consult marketing literature, research industry information, apply marketing theory and present their findings in a written report, with full assignment requirements available via Learn.UQ and discussed in classes prior to assessments commencing. A maximum word limit has been set in the guidelines available via Learn.UQ and any words submitted above the maximum limit will not be eligible for marking. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The written assignment must be submitted electronically through the Online Turnitin Submission on the Learn.UQ site, based on the specific instructions provided. Those who fail to follow the exact submission instructions will incur late penalties until submission is resolved.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Exam - during Exam Period (Central)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The aim of this final exam task is to increase knowledge of the marketing mix and demonstrate your understanding as an individual. This will include evaluation of theoretical knowledge and the ability to apply knowledge to real life marketing, using multiple choice questions and short answer questions. Further details of the final exam will be discussed in tutorials and lectures. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: Course overview and marketing process Overview of marketing and course expectations. Strategic marketing planning, customer value and competitive advantage. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
No tutorials There are no tutorials run during Week 1. Tutorials will commence in Week 2.
Marketing environments The internal and external marketing environment, including situational analysis. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Course overview and marketing planning Discussions of class schedules and assessment expectations. Application of marketing process and importance of customer value. Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Consumer behaviour Understanding the drivers of customer behaviour. During this week ALL students should also watch the Library Guide - accessed via course website. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
Marketing environments in action Understanding the influences of internal and external forces upon marketing. Learning outcomes: L02, L03
Segmentation and targeting (STP) Examining how brands gain competitive advantage through segmenting and targeting the marketplace. Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05

Customer behaviour in action Practical activities to examine and consider customer perspectives and influences on behaviour. Royal Queensland Show Public Holiday - Wednesday 14 Aug 2024 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes. Learning outcomes: L02, L03
Positioning (STP) Exploring how brands position their offering in the minds of consumers. Assessment 2 overview. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
STP in action - Segment and target Practical activities to segment, target and profile the most desirable customer. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
Product & Services Strategy Product decisions in the marketing mix. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
STP in action - Positioning Practical activities to understand positioning choices. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
Pricing Strategy Contribution of pricing to the marketing mix. Final exam overview. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
Product decisions in action Interactive activities to explore product decisions in the marketing mix. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
Place Strategy Distribution and marketing channel considerations. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
Pricing in action Interactive activities to explore pricing decisions in the marketing mix. Final A2 Q&A Learning outcomes: L04, L05
Promotion - IMC Creating value in relationships through personal selling, sales promotion and direct and digital marketing. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
Place in action Interactive activities to explore place decisions in the marketing mix. Learning outcomes: L04
In-Semester break No tutorials during mid-semester break.
Promotion - IMC Creating value in relationships through personal selling, sales promotion and direct and digital marketing. Learning outcomes: L04, L05
IMC Promotion in action Interactive activities to explore personal selling, sales promotion and digital/direct promotion decisions in the marketing mix. Learning outcomes: L04, L05

Market research and implementation Importance of marketing information and approaches to research, and consideration of evaluation, budgeting and implementation of marketing plans. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05

IMC Promotion in action Interactive activities to explore personal selling, sales promotion and digital/direct promotion decisions in the marketing mix. King's Birthday Public Holiday - Monday 7 Oct 2024 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes. Learning outcomes: L04, L05

Ethics and contemporary issues and final exam Overview of some of the major issues for ethical marketing and contemporary marketing managers. Discussion of the Final Exam approach. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Marketing research in action A practical session developing and conducting market research and considering marketing mix in execution. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Course review and exam preparation Final course review with a focus on exam preparation. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Marketing mix review Review of overall marketing mix, highlighting the unified nature. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
